title: What are the survey tools available to me as an instructor?
plain_text: Instructors have several different tools available for surveying students. Choose the best survey tool for your purpose based on what information you are gathering and how you will use the information obtained from the responses. Surveys or polls are a quick way to interact with students. You can use a survey to: Increase student engagement in your class. Check for understanding. Create a community in your class. Request feedback on course content. Gather feedback on teaching strategies. Allow students to share ideas. Create anonymous surveys. Canvas Survey within Canvas are created using Classic Quizzes and can be graded or ungraded. Surveys offer 12 types of questions to choose from. Microsoft Polls With the Microsoft Teams Polling tool, you can create polls before a Microsoft Teams meeting and launch the polls during it. Poll Everywhere is a survey tool that allows instructors to develop questions to poll students and obtain results in real time. Qualtrics Survey is available to all current Northeastern faculty, staff, and students.
body_html: Instructors have several different tools available for surveying students... [Full body content including Table of Contents, Matrix, and Tool Descriptions]

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.text: Instructors have several different tools available for surveying students. Choose the best survey tool for your purpose based on what information you are gathering and how you will use the information obtained from the responses. Surveys or polls are a quick way to interact with students. You can use a survey to: Increase student engagement, check for understanding, create community, request feedback, and allow students to share ideas.

sections.heading: Survey tool comparison matrix
sections.text: A matrix comparing Ease of use, real-time polling, chat features, anonymity, grading, Gradebook import, question types, randomization, accessibility, and result viewing across the four tools.

sections.heading: Canvas Survey in Quizzes
sections.text: Surveys within Canvas are created using Classic Quizzes and can be graded or ungraded. Offers 12 types of questions. Results appear in Gradebook when graded. Can be anonymous.

sections.heading: Microsoft Polls
sections.text: Using Microsoft Teams Polling tool to create polls before or during meetings. Ad-hoc polls can be created for feedback on the spot.

sections.heading: Poll Everywhere
sections.text: Real-time polling tool delivered via web or presentation software (PowerPoint, Keynote, Google Slides). Includes variety of question types and gamification options.

sections.heading: Qualtrics
sections.text: Powerful tool for complex surveys available to all Northeastern faculty, staff, and students. Includes extensive resources, templates, and accessibility checker.
